call_pattern: Unique ascending whistle pattern, repeated 2-5 times
behavioral_meaning: Signature whistle — individual identity call. Dolphin announcing its presence to pod members.
behavioral_response: Pod members respond with their own signature whistles. Group cohesion maintained.
translation_template: It's me — it's me! I'm over here. Where are you? This is my call, remember it.

intensity_modifiers.high: Where IS everyone?! It's ME! I'm HERE! Can anyone hear my whistle?! RESPOND!
intensity_modifiers.medium: Hey pod, it's me. Just checking in. I'm swimming over by the reef.
intensity_modifiers.low: Just me, passing through. All good.

call_pattern: Rapid click trains, 200-600 clicks per second
behavioral_meaning: Active echolocation for navigation and prey detection. Building acoustic map of environment.
behavioral_response: Dolphin adjusts swimming path based on echo returns.
translation_template: Scanning ahead... I can sense the bottom shape. Fish school 30 meters that way. Clear path ahead.

intensity_modifiers.high: Locked onto target! Fish school directly ahead! Accelerating — time to hunt!
intensity_modifiers.medium: Mapping the area. Some interesting echoes coming back from over there.
intensity_modifiers.low: Just a routine scan. Nothing unusual in the water ahead.

source: Au 1993; Herzing 2011; NOAA Marine Mammal Research

call_pattern: Burst-pulse sounds mixed with whistles during social play
behavioral_meaning: Social play and excitement. Often during chasing, leaping, or object play.
behavioral_response: Play partners respond with similar burst-pulse sounds. Play escalates.
translation_template: Catch me if you can! This is so fun! Let's jump together — WHEEE!

intensity_modifiers.high: THIS IS AMAZING! Chase me! Jump with me! Let's surf that wave! WOOHOO!
intensity_modifiers.medium: Having a good time. Come play with me — it's fun!
intensity_modifiers.low: Just goofing around a little. Feeling playful.

call_pattern: Long, complex melodic song sequence lasting 10-30 minutes, with repeated themes
behavioral_meaning: Male breeding song. Advertising fitness, attracting females, establishing dominance over rivals.
behavioral_response: Females assess and approach preferred singers. Rival males may counter-sing or challenge.
translation_template: I am here. I am strong. Listen to how long I can sing. Come find me.

intensity_modifiers.high: HEAR ME across the ocean! I am the STRONGEST singer here! My song carries for MILES! Come to ME!
intensity_modifiers.medium: Singing my song. I've refined every note. I hope someone worthy is listening.
intensity_modifiers.low: Practicing my phrases. Getting the melody right for the season.

source: Payne & McVay 1971; Garland et al. 2011; NOAA/MBARI

call_pattern: Social calls — short, varied non-song vocalizations between individuals
behavioral_meaning: Mother-calf bonding, pod coordination, social maintenance between non-singing individuals.
behavioral_response: Calf stays close to mother. Pod members coordinate movement and diving.
translation_template: Stay close to me, little one. We swim together. I'll keep you safe.

intensity_modifiers.high: Come HERE, baby! Stay RIGHT beside me! There may be danger — don't wander!
intensity_modifiers.medium: I'm right here with you. We'll dive together on my signal.
intensity_modifiers.low: Just us, swimming along. Nice and calm.

call_pattern: Deep 'jug-o-rum' bass call, repeated every few seconds
behavioral_meaning: Male advertisement call. Broadcasting fitness, body size (lower pitch = larger frog).
behavioral_response: Females approach preferred callers. Rival males may retreat or counter-call.
translation_template: I am right here, I am ready, and I am the loudest male in this pond tonight.

intensity_modifiers.high: LISTEN TO MY VOICE! I am the BIGGEST, LOUDEST frog here! Come to MY territory!
intensity_modifiers.medium: Calling from my spot. I'm a quality male with good territory. Come check me out.
intensity_modifiers.low: Just warming up my voice for the evening chorus.
